text: The surgeon injects vasopressin mixed with normal saline into the dissection plane around the left-sided broad ligament myoma, preparing for laparoscopic myomectomy.

The surgeon performs hydro dissection using a diluted vasopressin solution to create a clear dissection plane around the encapsulated myoma. The myoma is visible and encapsulated, with a myoma screw positioned on the left side of the port. The surgeon uses two myoma screws to provide symmetrical traction, retracting the myoma without a uterine manipulator.

The surgeon carefully separates the base of the myoma from surrounding structures, avoiding abrupt avulsion to prevent vessel and ureter damage. The myoma is easily extracted due to hydrodissection. A uterine manipulator is used, and the myoma is morselated using a morselator, a process that requires time.
The surgeon uses a morselator to gradually remove the left-sided broad ligament myoma in small pieces, a process that typically takes fifteen to twenty minutes.
